evidence: src/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError.ts uses module-level singleton state (`registeredNotifier`, `removeNotifierListener`) and a global `window` event channel (`reigh:error-notification`) to bridge core error handling and UI notification flow.

src/shared/hooks/useMobile.tsx and src/shared/components/MediaGallery/hooks/useMediaGalleryHandlers.ts both depend on `handleError`, which makes the global error event path a high-fan-in dependency surface.

src/domains/generation/types/index.ts is a broad barrel; consumers in src/shared/components/MediaGallery/hooks/useMediaGalleryController.ts and src/shared/components/MediaGallery/hooks/useMediaGalleryHandlers.ts import `Shot` from this hub rather than narrower type modules.

src/shared/components/MediaGallery/hooks/useMediaGalleryController.ts mainly re-groups and forwards nested context bags into src/shared/components/MediaGallery/hooks/useMediaGalleryHandlers.ts and `useMobileInteractions`, adding orchestration indirection with limited domain behavior at the controller boundary.

src/shared/components/MediaGallery/hooks/useMediaGalleryHandlers.ts and src/shared/components/MediaGalleryItem/components/ShotActions.tsx both maintain large argument/prop surface areas (multiple grouped state/action bundles), signaling ownership spread across adjacent layers.

UI composition patterns are inconsistent across neighboring files: raw `<button>` usage in src/pages/Home/components/InstallInstructionsModal.tsx and src/pages/Home/components/CreativePartnerPane.tsx alongside shared primitives in src/shared/components/ui/button.tsx and tooltip wrappers in src/shared/components/ui/tooltip.tsx.

`PersistedGenerationParams` includes `custom_aspect_ratio` (snake_case), but `GenerationParams` has no corresponding `customAspectRatio`, creating a naming-contract outlier at the same boundary mapper.
`PersistedGenerationParams` is described as storage-boundary snake_case, but also contains camelCase key `originalParams`, which breaks the stated convention in this file.

`src/domains/generation/types/index.ts` is a `types` barrel but re-exports runtime functions (`toGenerationParams`, `toPersistedGenerationParams`) alongside type exports, which is a convention outlier for a type-focused surface.
Both mappers return `{}` when input is null/non-object, silently normalizing invalid inputs instead of signaling malformed payloads.
`readNumber`/`readString` drop invalid values to `undefined` without surfaced context; parse failures are absorbed differently than explicit validation would indicate.
Unsupported known key `custom_aspect_ratio` is neither mapped into normalized shape nor surfaced as an error; it is only preserved indirectly via `extra`, which hides boundary mismatches.
Boundary translation logic is duplicated across `toGenerationParams` and `toPersistedGenerationParams` (two long field maps plus repeated `consumedKeys`), increasing seam drift risk.
The seam between storage and domain contracts is only partially explicit: one declared persisted field (`custom_aspect_ratio`) has no first-class domain handoff path.
The `types/index.ts` seam exposes both type contracts and transformation behavior from a single barrel, reducing clarity of integration boundary intent for importers.

Task modules repeat the same abstraction stack (validate single, validate batch, build params, create single task, create batch via Promise.allSettled) in multiple files: src/shared/lib/tasks/magicEdit.ts, src/shared/lib/tasks/zImageTurboI2I.ts, src/shared/lib/tasks/imageGeneration.ts.

useImageEditValue keeps dual interfaces (boundary model + legacy flat params) and conversion glue (toModelsFromLegacy/isBoundaryModels) despite the assigned caller using the boundary-model path (src/tools/edit-images/hooks/useInlineEditState.ts calling src/tools/edit-images/hooks/useImageEditValue.ts).

Video lightbox render orchestration constructs and forwards large nested parameter bags (panel/shotWorkflow/actions/buttonGroupProps) through seam helpers, increasing indirection cost (src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useVideoLightboxRenderModel.tsx, src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/components/layouts/LightboxLayout.tsx).
individualTravelSegment payload construction uses large mutable UnknownRecord objects with post-construction deletes and conditional mutation (e.g., deleting legacy/SVI keys after object creation).
travelBetweenImages payloadBuilder builds a single large Record<string, unknown> with many conditional spreads and mixed normalization/assembly responsibilities in one function.
These payload builders are intention-heavy and mechanically dense, making local edits high-risk despite comments.

API contracts for similar cross-boundary operations diverge: `generateVideo()` returns `{ success, error? }` instead of throwing (`src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotEditor/services/generateVideoService.ts:586`), while `loadClipDuration()` resolves fallback `durationSeconds: 0` on failure (`src/tools/join-clips/lib/clipManager/metadata.ts:11-24`) and `fetchLineageChain()` logs and returns `[]` (`src/shared/hooks/useLineageChain.ts:60-80`).

Two prop resolvers flatten nested sections via object spread (`resolveShotImagesEditorProps`, `resolveJoinClipsSettingsFormProps`), so key collisions are silently last-write-wins (`src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotImagesEditor/types.ts:168-181`, `src/shared/components/JoinClipsSettingsForm/types.ts:101-112`).

Legacy and current structure-video APIs coexist in one interface (`primaryStructureVideo*` fields plus `structureVideos[]` callbacks), creating parallel shapes for the same feature (`src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotImagesEditor/types.ts:76-136`).

Several files use high-comment, low-behavior patterns where comments restate obvious code paths (e.g., extensive explanatory blocks around tiny helpers in src/shared/components/ShotImageManager/utils/image-utils.ts and src/shared/lib/caseConversion.ts).

Pass-through wrappers add API surface without behavior (src/shared/components/ShotImageManager/utils/image-utils.ts:getAspectRatioStyle only delegates; src/shared/lib/supabaseTypeHelpers.ts:toJson is a single boundary cast wrapper).
`src/integrations/supabase/legacy/clientProxy.ts` still exports a compatibility proxy and warns at runtime, indicating active old/new client access paths.
`src/integrations/supabase/instrumentation/realtime/index.ts` and `window/index.ts` route through `*Legacy` functions with comments indicating removed manager code, leaving partial migration residue.
`src/integrations/supabase/config/env.ts` includes explicit migration contract comments with future remove-by date, confirming unfinished cleanup.

holistic_context.structure.directory_profiles['src/shared/hooks/'] shows file_count=92 (>10 mixed-concern threshold), avg_fan_in=6.1, and imports spanning infra and UI-adjacent dirs (src/integrations/supabase/=46, src/shared/lib/errorHandling/=40, src/shared/lib/queryKeys/=31).

Assigned hooks represent different domains in one flat package: useAutoTopup.ts (billing + supabase edge function), useBackgroundThumbnailGenerator.ts (media thumbnail pipeline), and useBatchImageLoading.ts (UI image presentation helper).

holistic_context.structure.directory_profiles['src/shared/components/'] shows file_count=78 with mixed responsibilities and bidirectional-like pressure with hooks via coupling_matrix ('src/shared/components/ → src/shared/hooks/' = 55).
Test placement is coherent for assigned hooks (src/shared/hooks/__tests__/ mirrors hook names), which partially offsets package sprawl but does not fix production directory mixing.

Image lightbox concerns are split into many helper hooks but still recomposed as a very large render model object with multiple grouped memo bundles and cross-cutting behavior assembly (src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/ImageLightbox.tsx:521-747), while the edit orchestrator exposes a very wide state/action surface (src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useImageEditOrchestrator.ts:470-540).

Timeline flow remains orchestration-heavy at both hook and component levels: useTimelineOrchestrator has a large prop contract and return contract (src/tools/travel-between-images/components/Timeline/hooks/useTimelineOrchestrator.ts:22-140), and Timeline.tsx still coordinates navigation, lightbox task loading, adapter wrappers, preloading, event wiring, and reset-frame logic in one component (src/tools/travel-between-images/components/Timeline.tsx:288-461).

Parallel UI pane implementations repeat structural mechanics (mobile backdrop interception, lock/open sync, delayed pointer-events enablement) in sibling panes instead of a shared pane-shell abstraction (src/shared/components/TasksPane/TasksPane.tsx:288-340 and src/shared/components/GenerationsPane/GenerationsPane.tsx:259-335), with additional lock/open coupling logic in context (src/shared/contexts/PanesContext.tsx:98-137).

Supabase edge handlers repeatedly inline the same request pipeline (env check, admin client creation, logger, method/body validation, auth, response patterns) across endpoints (supabase/functions/claim-next-task/index.ts:42-86, supabase/functions/get-task-output/index.ts:31-82, supabase/functions/get-completed-segments/index.ts:26-73), while some siblings additionally duplicate local CORS/jsonResponse wrappers (supabase/functions/setup-auto-topup/index.ts:6-19 and supabase/functions/complete-auto-topup-setup/index.ts:7-20).
`src/app/routes.tsx` uses a local-storage token presence check (`hasStoredSession`) for redirect decisions instead of authoritative session validation.
`src/features/account/hooks/useApiTokens.ts` mixes `supabase.auth.getUser()` and `supabase.auth.getSession()` as interchangeable authorization gates.
`src/features/settings/hooks/useApiKeys.ts` uses only `getUser()` for auth checks, creating sibling-hook divergence in auth contract.
`src/pages/Home/hooks/useHomeAuth.ts` performs additional session mutation (`setSession`) and redirects outside shared auth guard behavior.

findings.summary: Shared error handling relies on global browser event bus and singleton notifier state, creating hidden cross-layer coupling.
evidence: handleError.ts lines 25-35 and 42-70 define and manage a global `window` event channel plus module-level mutable notifier state.
useMobile.tsx line 14 and useMediaGalleryHandlers.ts lines 74, 81-85, 91-95, 102, 118 call `handleError`, so many feature hooks transitively depend on this global dispatch path.
findings.suggestion: Extract a small error-reporting port interface and inject notifier wiring at app bootstrap, so shared hooks depend on a pure reporter API instead of implicit global event state.

findings.summary: The generation types barrel acts as a cross-domain type hub, increasing coupling and broadening change blast radius.
evidence: domains/generation/types/index.ts re-exports many domain types from multiple files.
useMediaGalleryController.ts line 3 and useMediaGalleryHandlers.ts line 5 import `Shot` from the barrel rather than a narrower module path, reinforcing hub usage.
findings.suggestion: Prefer direct type imports from specific files (or a smaller scoped facade per subdomain) for internal feature code; reserve the broad barrel for explicit public API boundaries.

findings.summary: Media gallery behavior is split into pass-through controller and handler layers with large context bundles, weakening ownership clarity.
evidence: useMediaGalleryController.ts lines 13-41 define a large nested args contract, then lines 48-78 mostly forward/repackage inputs into other hooks.
useMediaGalleryHandlers.ts lines 16-41 and 48-65 unpack grouped contexts before implementing actions.
ShotActions.tsx uses four grouped prop objects (`selector`, `status`, `quickCreate`, `actions`), indicating boundary complexity propagating into UI components.
findings.suggestion: Re-cut boundaries around feature capabilities (e.g., shot-navigation actions, task-details actions, mobile tap logic) with smaller hook APIs and colocated state ownership to reduce pass-through layers.

findings.summary: Shared UI primitives and raw element usage are mixed inconsistently across adjacent features, blurring the intended design-system boundary.
evidence: button.tsx defines a shared button primitive, but InstallInstructionsModal.tsx line 68 and CreativePartnerPane.tsx use raw `<button>` elements for interactive actions.
Tooltip behavior is partially centralized in ui/tooltip.tsx, while CreativePartnerPane.tsx implements additional local tooltip controller state, resulting in mixed ownership of interaction policy.
findings.suggestion: Document and enforce UI boundary rules (when to use shared primitives vs local raw elements) and extract repeated interaction policies into shared composable hooks/components where reuse is expected.

findings.summary: Storage-boundary naming conventions drift within generation params contracts and mappers.
evidence: `PersistedGenerationParams` includes `custom_aspect_ratio` while `GenerationParams` lacks `customAspectRatio` and mapper support.
`PersistedGenerationParams` includes camelCase `originalParams` despite snake_case boundary convention documented in-file.
A `types` barrel (`index.ts`) re-exports runtime mapping functions, mixing type and behavior conventions.
findings.suggestion: Make boundary naming rules strict and symmetrical: add `customAspectRatio` to `GenerationParams`, map both directions explicitly, and align persisted naming (including `original_params` if boundary is strictly snake_case) or update the documented convention to match reality; split runtime mapper exports from a type-only barrel if that is the project standard.

findings.summary: Mapper failure modes are silent and inconsistent with explicit boundary error handling.
evidence: `toGenerationParams` and `toPersistedGenerationParams` return empty objects for invalid inputs instead of reporting malformed payloads.
Type coercion helpers (`readString`, `readNumber`) drop invalid values to `undefined` with no diagnostics.
Declared persisted fields without normalized mapping are silently pushed into `extra`, masking contract mismatches.
findings.suggestion: Adopt one boundary strategy consistently: either return a typed `Result`/error object for invalid payloads or throw a structured error at this layer; include explicit handling for known-but-invalid keys and reserve `extra` for truly unknown keys only.

findings.summary: The storage-domain translation seam is duplicated and prone to drift.
evidence: Two separate long mapper implementations each maintain overlapping field maps and consumed-key sets.
The seam already shows behavioral drift (`custom_aspect_ratio` declared but not first-class translated).
Single barrel exports both seam behavior and type contracts, making integration intent less explicit.
findings.suggestion: Centralize field mapping metadata (single source of truth) and generate both directions from it, including consumed-key computation; keep seam behavior exports in a dedicated mapper module entrypoint and keep `types` barrels type-only if consistent with project conventions.

findings.summary: Multiple task modules reimplement the same single-vs-batch builder pipeline with only task-specific field differences.
evidence: Each module defines paired parameter interfaces and paired validators, then batch functions that map params into per-task objects and call Promise.allSettled + processBatchResults.
magicEdit and zImageTurboI2I both generate per-item seeds, pass through mostly unchanged params, and delegate to a private single-task creator with near-identical control flow.
The abstraction is copy-shaped rather than reused, so adding cross-cutting behavior (telemetry/retry/validation policy changes) requires parallel edits.
findings.suggestion: Extract a shared task-creator template for the common pipeline (single-task create wrapper, batch fan-out strategy, result processing) and keep only task-specific param mapping in each module.

findings.summary: useImageEditValue maintains a legacy flat-params adapter path that adds conversion indirection on a now model-based call site.
evidence: useImageEditValue accepts a union of UseImageEditValueModels | UseImageEditValueLegacyParams and branches via isBoundaryModels/toModelsFromLegacy.
The assigned caller in useInlineEditState builds and passes grouped models (buildImageEditValueModels -> useImageEditValue), indicating the modern contract is already in use.
Keeping both shapes inflates type/API surface and increases interface dishonesty for new consumers.
findings.suggestion: Deprecate and remove the flat legacy signature (or move it to an explicit compatibility wrapper), leaving useImageEditValue with a single model-based contract.

findings.summary: Video lightbox integration relies on large prop-bag handoffs that obscure ownership across render, workflow, and layout layers.
evidence: useVideoLightboxRenderModel builds nested objects for panel, shotWorkflow, actions, and buttonGroupProps and forwards them into useLightboxWorkflowProps in one step.
VideoLightbox uses renderModel.layoutProps as a broad pass-through into LightboxLayout rather than narrow role-specific seams.
LightboxLayout then combines context reads and large prop surfaces (workflow/button groups/media controls), making upstream/downstream behavior tightly coupled.
findings.suggestion: Split the seam into smaller typed contracts (navigation contract, workflow actions contract, panel contract) with local selectors/adapters at each boundary so each layer owns fewer concerns.

findings.summary: Travel payload assembly mixes normalization, defaults, and mutation-heavy object surgery in large untyped records.
evidence: individualTravelSegment builds large UnknownRecord payloads, then mutates/deletes fields (e.g., SVI and legacy structure fields) after initial assembly.
payloadBuilder constructs one large Record<string, unknown> with many conditional spreads and compatibility branches, concentrating multiple concerns in one function.
Both files rely on broad unknown maps instead of explicit payload interfaces, reducing local guarantees and making changes error-prone.
findings.suggestion: Introduce explicit payload types and staged pure builders (normalize input -> compute defaults -> compose payload) that avoid post-hoc deletes and minimize mutable record manipulation.

findings.summary: Generation-related APIs expose inconsistent failure contracts (throw vs sentinel value vs success/error object), making callers handle errors non-uniformly.
evidence: `generateVideo()` returns `{ success: false, error }` via `failureResult()` rather than throwing (`generateVideoService.ts:186-189,586-709`).
`loadClipDuration()` catches and returns `{ durationSeconds: 0 }` (`metadata.ts:17-24`).
`fetchLineageChain()` catches Supabase errors via `handleError` and returns `[]` (`useLineageChain.ts:60-80`).
`getPrimaryTaskIdForGeneration()` throws on query error (`generationTaskBridge.ts:39-41`).
findings.suggestion: Define one explicit error contract for this subsystem (prefer typed Result or typed exceptions) and apply it consistently across service/hook boundaries, with adapters only at UI edges.

findings.summary: Prop resolver helpers flatten multiple prop groups with spread-merges, which can silently override same-named keys.
evidence: `resolveShotImagesEditorProps()` merges four sections (`displayOptions`, `imageState`, `editActions`, `shotWorkflow`) into one object without conflict checks (`ShotImagesEditor/types.ts:174-180`).
`resolveJoinClipsSettingsFormProps()` similarly merges `clipSettings`, `motionConfig`, `uiState` (`JoinClipsSettingsForm/types.ts:106-111`).
findings.suggestion: Keep sectioned props at boundaries (no flattening), or add compile-time collision guards using mapped-type key-disjoint checks before merge.

findings.summary: Shot image editor types expose both legacy single-video and new multi-video structure APIs simultaneously, creating ambiguous integration paths.
evidence: `ShotImagesEditorImageState` includes `primaryStructureVideo*` fields and also `structureVideos?: StructureVideoConfigWithMetadata[]` (`ShotImagesEditor/types.ts:76-87`).

`ShotImagesEditorEditActions` includes both `onPrimaryStructureVideoInputChange(...)` (6+ args) and array-based `onAddStructureVideo/onUpdateStructureVideo/onSetStructureVideos` (`ShotImagesEditor/types.ts:120-135`).
`buildStructureGuidance()` consumes only array-based shape (`generateVideoService.ts:82-124`), indicating single-video path is legacy but still publicly exposed.
findings.suggestion: Complete migration to one structure-video contract and isolate legacy adapters in a dedicated compatibility module, not in the main editor prop API.

findings.summary: The video generation seam is overloaded with orchestration, data access, translation, and submission concerns in one module.
evidence: Module handles query synchronization (`waitForPendingMutations`), DB fetching (`fetchFreshShotGenerations`), filtering/translation (`buildImagePayload`, `buildTimelinePairConfig`), model selection/validation, and task submit (`createTravelBetweenImagesTask`) in one flow (`generateVideoService.ts:227-709`).
The request body builder (`buildTravelRequestBodyV2`) requires nested parameter groups and many conditional fields (`generateVideoService.ts:465-556`), signaling seam complexity.
Adjacent modules already define separate transformation/contract utilities (`generationTransformers.ts`, `orchestrationContract.ts`), but this seam partially reinvents local mapping.
findings.suggestion: Split into explicit collaborators: `ShotGenerationRepository`, `PairConfigBuilder`, `TravelRequestBuilder`, and `TaskSubmissionService`; keep `generateVideo()` as thin orchestration only.

findings.summary: Write serialization and cache invalidation lifecycle are coordinated through separate, inconsistent primitives, increasing seam friction.
evidence: `timelineWriteQueue` exposes queue depth/phase events and timeout-wrapped write execution (`timelineWriteQueue.ts:54-190`), while invalidation uses independent timeout maps and scheduled callbacks (`useGenerationInvalidation.ts:68-114`).
Invalidation entry points have mixed contracts (`Promise<void>`, timeout handle, fire-and-forget query invalidation) for near-identical lifecycle intent (`useGenerationInvalidation.ts:96-120`).
Cache utils add another separate orchestration layer (`updateAllShotsCaches`, `rollbackShotsCaches`, `cancelShotsQueries`) without shared lifecycle primitive (`cacheUtils.ts:31-97`).
findings.suggestion: Introduce a shared write-lifecycle coordinator abstraction that unifies serialize-write, cancel/refetch, and delayed invalidation semantics behind one subsystem API.

findings.summary: Legacy and canonical schema paths are both still active in settings and task-id resolution.
evidence: LegacyProjectImageSettings keeps multiple @deprecated fields (selectedReferenceId, styleReference*, selectedLorasByTextModel) and is still part of accepted input shape via ProjectImageSettingsInput.
getSourceTaskId still accepts legacy key names (orchestrator_task_id, task_id) in addition to source_task_id.
findings.suggestion: Add a migration cutoff plan: instrument reads of deprecated fields/legacy task-id keys, report usage, then remove fallback keys and narrow accepted types to canonical fields only once usage reaches zero.

findings.summary: Comment-heavy pass-through helpers add ceremony without meaningful behavior.
evidence: getAspectRatioStyle in image-utils.ts only forwards projectAspectRatio to resolveAspectRatioStyle with no translation or guard logic.
toJson in supabaseTypeHelpers.ts is a single cast wrapper (`value as unknown as Json`) while carrying a long explanatory block.
caseConversion.ts contains extensive tutorial-style comments around straightforward key conversion logic.
findings.suggestion: Remove or inline pure pass-through helpers where they do not enforce policy, and trim comments to non-obvious constraints (why/contract) instead of narrating what the next line does.

findings.summary: Core helpers swallow failures, reducing precision of edge-case handling.
evidence: preloader.ts catches queue/add failures and does nothing, so bad URLs/network failures are indistinguishable from normal best-effort skips.
supabaseSession.ts returns null for all JSON/localStorage parse errors, collapsing corrupted-session errors into the same return path as signed-out users.
findings.suggestion: Keep non-throwing behavior but expose explicit failure signals (debug logger, error counters, or discriminated result types) so callers can distinguish expected empty states from malformed/failed states.

findings.summary: The shared hooks root is overloaded with unrelated billing, media-processing, and UI-loading hooks, reducing navigability and ownership clarity.
evidence: Directory profile for src/shared/hooks/ has 92 production files, exceeding the prompt’s mixed-concern split threshold (>10).

useAutoTopup.ts couples to billing/auth infrastructure (supabase client, invokeWithTimeout('setup-auto-topup'), credit query keys), while useBackgroundThumbnailGenerator.ts handles background video thumbnail jobs, and useBatchImageLoading.ts is a thin presentation helper around progressive image loading.
These files are all first-class exports from the same flat directory despite distinct change boundaries.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: (1) Create target folders `src/shared/hooks/billing/`, `src/shared/hooks/media/`, and `src/shared/hooks/ui-image/` plus mirrored test folders under `src/shared/hooks/__tests__/billing|media|ui-image/`. (2) Move in order: `useBatchImageLoading.ts` first, then `useBackgroundThumbnailGenerator.ts`, then `useAutoTopup.ts` to minimize cross-cut edits while stabilizing imports. (3) Update imports with `rg -l "@/shared/hooks/use(BatchImageLoading|BackgroundThumbnailGenerator|AutoTopup)" src` and replace to new paths; move/update the three corresponding test files. (4) Validate with `pnpm vitest run src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useBatchImageLoading.test.ts src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useBackgroundThumbnailGenerator.test.ts src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useAutoTopup.test.ts` and `pnpm tsc --noEmit`.

findings.summary: AppErrorBoundary is an application-shell boundary object stored in a generic shared widget directory, blurring architectural layering.
evidence: AppErrorBoundary.tsx implements top-level crash containment and full-page recovery behavior, which is app-shell responsibility rather than reusable shared widget behavior.
Its co-location with domain/UI controls (ActiveLoRAsDisplay.tsx, AspectRatioSelector.tsx) indicates shared/components root is acting as a catch-all instead of clear layer boundaries.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: (1) Create `src/app/components/error/` as target folder for app-shell boundaries. (2) Move `AppErrorBoundary.tsx` there before moving feature controls, so bootstrap imports are isolated first. (3) Update imports via `rg -l "@/shared/components/AppErrorBoundary" src` and replace with `@/app/components/error/AppErrorBoundary`. (4) Validate with `pnpm vitest run` (or focused app bootstrap tests if present) and `pnpm tsc --noEmit`.

findings.summary: Generation/editor-specific controls are placed in shared/components root instead of capability-focused subpackages.
evidence: Directory profile for src/shared/components/ reports 78 files with mixed concerns, matching flat-directory overload conditions.

ActiveLoRAsDisplay.tsx embeds LoRA-specific behavior (trigger words, strength controls, model-media preview logic), and AspectRatioSelector.tsx is a generation/editor configuration control; both are feature-oriented rather than generic primitives.
Keeping these in top-level shared/components dilutes discoverability of generation/editing feature surfaces.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: (1) Create `src/shared/components/generation-controls/` (or `src/features/generation/components/` if feature packages are preferred). (2) Move `AspectRatioSelector.tsx` and `ActiveLoRAsDisplay.tsx` together after AppErrorBoundary relocation, preserving local cohesion; then move or co-locate their close collaborators (e.g., visualizer) in the same package. (3) Update imports with `rg -l "@/shared/components/(ActiveLoRAsDisplay|AspectRatioSelector)" src` and rewrite to the new folder path. (4) Validate with `pnpm vitest run` for impacted component/hook tests and `pnpm tsc --noEmit`.

findings.summary: Image lightbox design still centralizes too many responsibilities behind broad state bundles and orchestration outputs.
evidence: ImageLightbox composes core/media/variants/navigation/action workflows plus panel visibility/layout concerns in one render-model path (ImageLightbox.tsx:544-736).

useImageEditOrchestrator returns a very wide aggregate object spanning mode toggles, stroke editing, reposition transforms, prompt/lora/model settings, and generation status flags (useImageEditOrchestrator.ts:470-540).
EditModePanel consumes that broad orchestration surface, reinforcing a high-coupling API between display and edit subsystems.
findings.suggestion: Split orchestration by explicit mode domains (inpaint, magic-edit, reposition, img2img) with narrow typed adapters per panel; keep lightbox render model focused on layout and navigation only.

findings.summary: Timeline behavior is distributed across large component and large hook contracts, creating unclear ownership boundaries.
evidence: useTimelineOrchestrator defines a very large input and output interface combining drag/drop, zoom, selection, endpoint editing, and structure-video flows (useTimelineOrchestrator.ts:22-140).
Timeline.tsx still handles lightbox task lookup, adjacent segment composition, add-to-shot adapters, image preloading, app-event subscriptions, and frame reset logic together (Timeline.tsx:304-419).
useTimelineCore independently owns positioning/prompt/metadata mutations and query invalidation orchestration (useTimelineCore.ts:664-719), creating overlapping orchestration layers.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a timeline application-service boundary: one domain hook for mutation/query operations, one interaction hook for UI gestures, and keep Timeline.tsx as a thin composition/view layer.

findings.summary: Two different ShotSelector components implement overlapping selection/create/navigation behavior with diverged APIs and UX contracts.
evidence: There are two separate `ShotSelector` implementations with different prop shapes and interaction models (shared/ShotSelector.tsx vs ImageGenerationForm/components/ShotSelector.tsx).

Both own shot selection plus auxiliary actions (create/jump/clear), but one uses popover+command search and quick-create semantics while the other uses select+inline actions, increasing behavioral drift risk.
Callers in image generation paths must depend on form-local selector semantics instead of a shared domain selector contract.
findings.suggestion: Converge on a single domain-level selector primitive with optional feature flags/slots for create/visit/clear actions; keep style variants separate from behavior contracts.

findings.summary: Pane interaction mechanics are repeated across sibling panes instead of being modeled once.
evidence: Both pane components implement near-parallel backdrop event swallowing (`onTouchStart`/`onPointerDown` with preventDefault/stopPropagation + closePane) and pane-control-tab wiring (TasksPane.tsx:324-363, GenerationsPane.tsx:259-315).

Both implement animation/pointer-event guard behavior in UI-layer code rather than through shared pane lifecycle primitives (TasksPane.tsx:305-317; GenerationsPane.tsx inner wrapper pointer-event gating).
PanesContext additionally carries lock/open synchronization logic specific to tasks pane behavior, indicating distributed pane state policy (PanesContext.tsx:98-137).
findings.suggestion: Create a shared `PaneShell` abstraction with standardized backdrop, pointer-gating, and open/lock synchronization hooks, then keep each pane focused on content and pane-specific actions.

findings.summary: Supabase edge handlers repeat the same request lifecycle structure, leading to copy-driven endpoint design.
evidence: Multiple handlers inline the same sequence: env validation, createClient, logger creation, method gate, body parse, authenticateRequest, and error response mapping (claim-next-task.ts:42-86; get-task-output.ts:31-82; get-completed-segments.ts:26-73).
Auto-topup endpoints locally redefine near-identical CORS/jsonResponse scaffolding (setup-auto-topup.ts:6-19; complete-auto-topup-setup.ts:7-20).
This repetition creates structural inconsistency in parsing details (`req.text` + JSON.parse vs `req.json`) and response style (`new Response` vs helper), indicating missing shared endpoint skeleton.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a shared edge-function handler factory (method/body/auth/env/logger policy) and unify per-endpoint logic to pure business handlers.

findings.summary: Mutation/query paths mix explicit project scoping and ID-only access, creating inconsistent authorization boundaries in client data access.
evidence: `useGenerationMutations` updates/deletes `generations` and `generation_variants` by `id` only (no project/user predicate).
`useShotImagesEditorCallbacks` builds `idsToDelete` then executes `supabase.from('generations').delete().in('id', idsToDelete)` without project scoping.
`generationTaskBridge` fetches task mappings via generation/task IDs only, and prefetches single tasks by ID.
`useTasks` explicitly scopes listings by project (`eq/in('project_id', ...)`), showing a different authorization/scoping pattern for sibling task data access.
`individualTravelSegment` child-resolution queries match `parent_generation_id` + pair/order without project guard in-query.
findings.suggestion: Standardize data-access authorization posture: introduce shared helpers for generation/task reads and mutations that require project context and append `project_id` predicates where schema allows; for ID-only operations, add a pre-check enforcing ownership/project match before delete/update. Apply this consistently across generation/task hooks.

findings.summary: Legacy Supabase compatibility layers remain in active runtime paths
evidence: `legacy/clientProxy.ts` still exports `supabase` proxy and runtime deprecation warning.
Both instrumentation entry points call `*Legacy` functions and carry migration commentary about removed managers.
`env.ts` includes explicit remove-by migration contract for legacy symbol cleanup.
findings.suggestion: Complete migration in stages: 1) remove remaining imports of `legacy/clientProxy` and add compile-time ban via ESLint/no-restricted-imports; 2) inline final instrumentation implementations and delete `install*Legacy` wrappers; 3) remove migration contract comments and dead toggles once no legacy references remain; 4) run `pnpm typecheck && pnpm test src/integrations/supabase` to validate behavior.

findings.summary: Sibling modules use incompatible auth checks and trust boundaries
evidence: `routes.tsx` redirect loader trusts cached token presence (`hasStoredSession`) rather than session validity.
`useApiTokens.ts` uses both `getUser()` and `getSession()` checks for same feature area.
`useApiKeys.ts` uses only `getUser()` and has different auth failure behavior from token hooks.
`useHomeAuth.ts` performs direct session mutation and redirect policy outside shared guard path.
findings.suggestion: Define one frontend auth gate contract (recommended: `getSession()` + shared helper) and apply it in all auth-sensitive hooks/routes. Create a small `ensureAuthenticated()` utility under `src/integrations/supabase/auth/`, migrate route loader + API key/token hooks to it, then add targeted tests for unauthenticated/expired-session paths.

findings.summary: Core flows mix handled errors with silent catch blocks
evidence: `useHomeAuth.ts` has several `catch {}` blocks that suppress referral/localStorage/session side-effect failures.
`useAdjacentPagePreloader.ts` ignores prefetch errors without reporting context.
`App.tsx` suppresses `getNetworkStatusManager()` initialization errors entirely.
findings.suggestion: Adopt a single non-intrusive reporting rule for non-critical failures: log via `handleError(..., { showToast: false })` with stable contexts. Replace empty catches in these files with that rule and add one helper (`reportNonCritical`) to avoid repetitive boilerplate.

findings.summary: Home auth abstraction bundles unrelated responsibilities
evidence: `useHomeAuth.ts` handles token parsing, session writes, redirect policy, referral RPC, and auth subscriptions in one hook.
`HomePage.tsx` depends on this plus many additional hooks, increasing orchestration indirection at page level.
`usePaneState.ts` duplicates pane transition logic across three parallel state sets.
findings.suggestion: Split by responsibility: 1) `useOAuthHashSessionRestore`, 2) `useStandaloneAuthRedirect`, 3) `useAuthReferralFinalize`; keep `useHomeAuth` as thin composition only. For panes, replace parallel booleans with a discriminated union state machine (`activePane: 'none'|'creative'|'philosophy'|'examples'`, `phase: 'idle'|'opening'|'closing'`).

findings.summary: Home feature directories are overloaded and mix multiple concerns
evidence: `holistic_context.structure.directory_profiles['src/pages/Home/components/']` reports `file_count: 26` with mixed concern imports and low cohesion.
`src/pages/Home/hooks/` has 18 files covering unrelated concerns (auth, media preload, theme, pane UI) without subfolders.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: Stage 1 create target folders `src/pages/Home/sections`, `src/pages/Home/panes`, `src/pages/Home/media`, `src/pages/Home/install`, `src/pages/Home/auth`, `src/pages/Home/runtime`; Stage 2 move files in order (auth/runtime hooks first, then install/motion components, then pane/hero sections); Stage 3 update imports using `rg -l "src/pages/Home/(components|hooks)/" src | xargs sed -i '' -E 's#src/pages/Home/components/#src/pages/Home/sections/#g'` (repeat per folder mapping); Stage 4 validate with `pnpm typecheck && pnpm test src/pages/Home` and smoke-run routes.

findings.summary: Pane behavior is implemented as a fragile boolean matrix
evidence: `usePaneState.ts` maintains many booleans for open/close/opening/closing/button animating for three panes, with duplicated timeout-driven transitions.
`HomePage.tsx` and `HeroSection.tsx` consume this state shape broadly, increasing branching and prop coupling.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a compact reducer-based state model (`activePane`, `animatingPane`, `transitionPhase`) and action events (`OPEN_PANE`, `CLOSE_PANE`, `ANIMATION_DONE`). Keep timer handling in one transition scheduler hook to remove repeated `setTimeout` blocks.

findings.summary: Verbose/stale comment ceremony obscures current intent in Supabase runtime code
evidence: Multiple comments restate obvious code operations (for example repeated 'Clear pending intents', 'Set new debounce timer') instead of documenting non-obvious rationale.
Comments like 'removed for simplicity' and legacy delegation notes are retained after implementation shifts, reducing signal-to-noise.
findings.suggestion: Prune explanatory repetition and keep only why-level comments (external constraints, race-condition rationale, invariants). Remove stale migration narration from these files after migration cleanup, and enforce a short comment policy in this subsystem (`why` only, no flow narration).
